title: Belloa burkartii
taxonomy: Belloa burkartii is classified in the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family comprises over 23,000 species, including many common and ornamental plants. Belloa burkartii is a member of the tribe Mutisieae, which is a group of plants characterized by their daisy-like flowers and composite inflorescences. The genus Belloa is part of the subfamily Mutisioideae, which is a group of plants native to the temperate regions of South America. Belloa burkartii is a specific species within the genus Belloa.

intro: Belloa burkartii, also known as the "Andean yerba", is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The leaves of Belloa burkartii have been used in traditional medicine for their reported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Belloa burkartii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Belloa burkartii is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, particularly those related to inflammation and oxidative stress.
